title: Nominalisation & Noun Phrases
topics: nominalisation · complex noun phrases · academic style · conciseness
description: Nominalizace — jak přetvořit slovesa na podstatná jména pro formálnější styl.
notes: ✓ Nominalisation = turning verbs/adjectives into nouns for formal writing
Formal: 'The decision to conduct an investigation was made.'
⚠️  Overuse makes text heavy — balance nominalisation with active verbs
✓ Complex noun phrases: 'a significant reduction in carbon emissions'
✓ Pre-modifiers stack before noun: 'a long-awaited government-backed renewable energy initiative'

examples.en: The improvement in results was significant.
examples.cz: Zlepšení výsledků bylo výrazné. (vs 'Results improved significantly.')

examples.en: The analysis of the data revealed unexpected patterns.
The implementation of the new policy was delayed.
Her achievement of a distinction was remarkable.
examples.cz: Její výsledek s vyznamenáním byl pozoruhodný.

quiz.sentence: We need to ___ the situation. → The ___ of the situation is needed. (analyse)
They decided to reduce costs. → The ___ to reduce costs was taken.
Scientists discovered a new species. → The ___ of a new species was announced.
The company improved performance. → A significant ___ in performance was observed.

tasks: 1. Rewrite these sentences using nominalisation: 'They investigated the problem.' / 'The government decided to act.'
2. Take a paragraph from your writing and increase formality using nominalisation
3. Find 5 complex noun phrases in a quality English newspaper article
